name: ilspy
description: Agent-friendly .NET decompilation via ilspy-cli (akiselev/ilspy-cli) — type/method listing, single-method decompilation, regex search, assembly metadata, and .NET vs native detection using the ILSpy ICSharpCode.Decompiler engine.
prerequisites: Rust 1.70+ (cargo on PATH, for source build)

install.notes: ilspy-cli is a standalone repo at github.com/akiselev/ilspy-cli (previously a subcrate of akiselev/ghidra-cli). The build script runs `dotnet publish` on the C# bridge (IlSpyBridge.dll). After install, copy `<repo>/target/release/bridge/` to a stable location and set `ILSPY_BRIDGE_DIR` so the bridge survives a `cargo clean`.

integration_test.description: Verifies .NET runtime is present and the IlSpyBridge.dll loads successfully.

post_install_hints: Install .NET 8 SDK if missing (Windows: `winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8`; macOS: `brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`; Linux: see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/install/linux).

Stage the bridge DLL to a stable location: copy `<repo-clone>/target/release/bridge/` to e.g. `~/tools/ilspy-bridge`, then set `ILSPY_BRIDGE_DIR` to that path (persist with `setx` on Windows or shell rc on Unix).
Run `ilspy doctor` — checks .NET runtime + bridge DLL load.
